hypotheses.hypothesis: entity was deleted but later referenced
hypotheses.evidence: No observed prior create/delete lifecycle for 5594 in bounded trace.

hypotheses.hypothesis: entity index was reused with a new serial
hypotheses.evidence: No observed registered entity at index 5594 before failure, so serial reuse is not demonstrated.
Failure is lookup by absent index, not a wrong-serial hit.

hypotheses.hypothesis: packet-local entity state is skipped after recoverable error
hypotheses.evidence: Task 035 whole-packet skip caused cascading warnings; update-payload skip preserves the invalid update but later baseline failure still blocks continuation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: parser incorrectly aborts an entire packet after one invalid entity
hypotheses.evidence: Default handler throws on missing update reference; opt-in skip isolates the invalid update before the next blocker.
Failure occurs before serializer lookup because entity registry lookup returns null.
Failure is UPDATE, not CREATE baseline lookup.
Decoded index is reproducible and has payload size; no independent bitstream proof of wrong decoding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: replay packet corruption or unsupported protocol data
hypotheses.evidence: A missing update reference may indicate unsupported protocol semantics or malformed packet ordering; further protocol-level evidence would be needed.
